title: First Impressions (comic story)

[[2 BBY/Legends|2]]-[[1 BBY/Legends|1 BBY]]<ref>''First Impressions'' depicts [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa's]] first visit to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] with her (adopted) father, Senator [[Bail Prestor Organa|Bail Organa]] of [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa's]] birth to [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. From ''[[Children of the Jedi]]'' it is stipulated that Leia formally joined the [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Imperial Senate]] at 18 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] of age. Hence, the timeline of ''First Impressions'' is no earlier than [[2 BBY/Legends|2 BBY]] as dated from Leia's birth year.</ref>
|series=

|followed by=
}}
''First Impressions'' is a comic appearing in ''[[Star Wars Tales 15]]'' and collected in ''[[Star Wars Tales Volume 4]]''.

==Plot summary==
[[Senator/Legends|Senator]] [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Prestor Organa]], preparing to hand his [[Imperial Senate/Legends|Senate]] seat over to [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]], brings her to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]] for the first time. Her father allows her to go off into the city with [[Alderaan Guard/Legends|two guards]]. At first entranced by what she sees, Leia then witnesses a [[Caamasi/Legends|Caamasi]] being arrested by two [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]] for simply trying to buy a drink at a cafe. When she tries to intervene on his behalf, the stormtroopers nearly arrest her as well.

Back at the apartment, Leia fumes to Bail, and says that she'll bring it up to [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] when she meets him at a reception being held at the [[Imperial Palace/Legends|Imperial Palace]] that night. Bail tries to dissuade her, saying that doing so now would jeopardize [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]] when, once she is a senator, she will have the ability and power to try to make changes. Leia refuses to be deterred.

At the Palace, Leia is appalled at how deferential and pandering everyone is to the Emperor, which only hardens her resolve to bring up the issue to him. When they finally meet face to face, however, Leia is struck by the pure evil radiating from him, and is unable to do anything more than mumble thanks to his compliments as [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] watches.

Following the reception, Bail assures Leia that it is nothing to be ashamed of, as that is a common reaction to seeing Palpatine, especially for the first time, and that to make changes, one must be subtle. He tells her how proud he is of her, and relates a piece of good news - he has arranged for the Caamasi she saw arrested to be released. Happy, Leia goes to meet him, and the story ends with him introducing himself as [[Eg'ros Akala]].

==Continuity==
The age of Leia is somewhat ill-defined in ''First Impressions'', as she is still a teenager with a very naïve demeanour and not the hard-headed [[Alliance to Restore the Republic|Rebel]] yet seen in ''[[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|A New Hope]]''. [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]]'s and Leia's well-defined birth year is [[19 BBY/Legends|19 BBY]]. The same year the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] ends with [[Order 66/Legends|Order 66]] achieving the murders of nearly all the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] and the [[Rise of the Empire era|Rise of the Empire]] with the ascension of [[Palpatine/Legends|Emperor Palpatine]]. ''[[Children of the Jedi (novel)|Children of the Jedi]]'' established that Leia was 18 [[Standard year/Legends|years]] old when she formally replaced her (adopted) father Bail Organa as the Senator for Alderaan in the Imperial Senate. The events in ''First Impressions'' would have to place Leia as being around 16 to 17 years old and thus as early as 3-2 BBY; however, 1 BBY is a problematic timeline without more corroborating evidence as to how long Leia spent in the Senate prior to joining the Rebellion and the events in ''[[Rogue One: A Star Wars Story|Rogue One]]'' that lead to the events of ''A New Hope''. 

==Legacy==
The events of this story were referenced in ''[[Star Wars: The Ultimate Visual Guide]]'' and the new canon novel ''[[Moving Target: A Princess Leia Adventure]]''.
